immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-ICU-or-MCU local admission duration summary retaining elapsed days from the current local critical-care discharge boundary to the first later ICU or MCU local admission observed for the same patient in the captured source.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_days_to_next_icu_mcu_admission must preserve ICU/MCU local-admission target grain, local critical-care discharge anchoring, first-later ICU/MCU local admission selection, source-observed follow-up scope, and explicit null/status semantics. Hospital admission readmission timing, ICU-only timing, same-hospitalization ICU return flags, and all-system utilization claims require separate identities or separate approval.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: start at the current ICU/MCU local admission discharge or local stay-end boundary
summary_contract.window_end_rule: end at the first later ICU/MCU local admission start boundary observed for the same subject
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: one retained row per ICU/MCU local admission with subject_id, stay_id, and local discharge boundary present
summary_contract.selection_rule: within the same subject, select the first later ICU/MCU local admission whose admission/start offset is strictly after the current local discharge/end offset
summary_contract.tie_break_rule: order candidate future local admissions by admission/start offset and local stay identifier after enforcing strict post-discharge timing
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not synthesize local admissions outside the source admission table; retain a null value for source local admissions with no later ICU/MCU local admission observed
summary_contract.partial_window_action: retain an explicit unresolved status when the current local discharge boundary is missing; do not reinterpret missing future events as zero duration

canonical_representation.range_role_note: The retained value is semantically nonnegative. Long observed gaps can occur because this is an observed future critical-care-utilization duration inside the captured source; the plausible band is a review cue, not an exclusion rule.

null_semantics.null_meaning: No later ICU/MCU local admission was observed after current local discharge in the captured source, or the current discharge boundary was unresolved under a database-specific status.
null_does_not_mean: the patient was proven never to require future critical care
the patient had zero days to next ICU/MCU admission
the patient was followed in all external hospital systems
the row is a hospital readmission measure
null_semantics.status_column_policy: A status column is required because null retained values need to distinguish no later observed local admission from unresolved missing current discharge boundaries when such missing boundaries exist.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external outcome and utilization terminology as semantic context only; govern this variable primarily by local critical-care admission/discharge boundaries, future local admission search rule, local-admission grain, and day-unit representation.
external_alignment.note: This is a source-observed local critical-care utilization duration, not a direct coded clinical event and not a binary readmission indicator.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing from ICU/MCU local-admission grain to hospital-admission encounter grain
changing from ICU/MCU inclusive timing to ICU-only timing
changing from time-to-next local admission to a binary readmission flag
changing from first later local admission after discharge to same-stay transfer or same-hospitalization return
claiming all-system critical-care follow-up rather than source-observed follow-up inside the captured database
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same ICU/MCU local-admission discharge anchor and future-admission search rule
adding or refining status columns for a database with incomplete discharge boundaries while keeping retained duration semantics

allowed_database_specific_variation: local identifiers used to normalize patient and local critical-care admission keys
database-specific names for ICU/MCU local admission and discharge offsets
database-specific evidence proving whether local future admissions are source-observed only or more complete within a health system
database-specific status handling when discharge timestamps are missing, if explicitly mapped
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing hospital readmission timing as std_days_to_next_icu_mcu_admission
publishing ICU-only timing under this ICU/MCU-inclusive identity when the intended database scope excludes MCU/HDU-equivalent admissions
publishing a binary 30-day readmission indicator as this duration variable
treating null retained values as zero days
claiming complete all-system future critical-care follow-up without database-specific proof

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_icu_mcu_admission outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_days_to_next_icu_mcu_admission outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: MIMIC-IV-3.1 is not mapped under this ICU/MCU-inclusive identity because MIMIC's current approved sibling is ICU-only std_days_to_next_icu_admission.
AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 remains not approved for std_days_to_next_hospital_admission because a true hospital-admission encounter bridge is not proven.
